passages.title: Caral: an ancient South American city
passages.source_html:
Caral: an ancient South American city


A


Huge earth and rock mounds rise out of the desert of the Supe Valley near the coast of Peru in South America. These immense mounds appear simply to be part of the geographical landscape in this arid region squeezed between the Pacific Ocean and the Andes mountains. But looks deceive. These are actually human-made pyramids. Strong evidence indicates they are the remains of a city known as Caral that flourished nearly 5,000 years ago. If true, it would be the oldest known urban center in the Americas and among the most ancient in the world.


B


Research undertaken by Peruvian archaeologist Ruth Shady suggests that the 150-acre complex of pyramids, plazas and residential buildings was a thriving metropolis when Egypt's great pyramids were still being built. Though discovered in 1905, for years Caral attracted little attention, largely because archaeologists believed the structures were fairly recent. But the monumental scale of the pyramids had long interested Shady, who began excavations at the site in 1996, about 22 kilometers from the coast and 190 kilometers north of Peru's capital city of Lima.


C


Shady and her crew searched for broken remains of the pots and containers that most such sites contain. Not finding any only made her more excited; it meant Caral could be what archaeologists term pre-ceramic, that is, existing before the advent in the area of pot-firing techniques. Shady's team undertook the task of excavating Piramide Mayor, the largest of the pyramids. After carefully clearing away many hundreds of years' worth of rubble and sand, they identified staircases, walls covered with remnants of colored plaster, and brickwork. In the foundations, they found the remains of grass-like reeds woven into bags. The original workers, she surmised, must have filled these bags with stones from a nearby quarry and laid them atop one another inside retaining walls, gradually giving rise to the pyramids immense structure. Shady had samples of the reeds subjected to radiocarbon dating and found that the reeds were 4,600 years old. This evidence indicated that Caral was, in fact, more than 1,000 years older than what had previously been thought to be the oldest urban center in the Americas.


D

 

What amazed archaeologists was not just the age, but the complexity and scope of Caral. Piramide Mayor alone covers an area nearly the size of four football fields and is 18 meters tall. A nine-meter-wide staircase rises from a circular plaza at the foot of the pyramid, passing over three terraced levels until it reaches the top. Thousands of manual laborers would have been needed to build such a project, not counting the many architects. craftsmen, and managers. Shady's team found the remains of a large amphitheater, containing almost 70 musical instruments made of bird and deer bones. Clearly, music played an important role in Caral's society Around the perimeter of Caral are a series of smaller mounds and various buildings. These indicate a hierarchy of living arrangements: large, well-kept rooms atop pyramids for the elite, ground-level quarters for craftsmen, and shabbier outlying dwellings for workers.


E


But why had Caral been built in the first place? Her excavations convinced Shady that Caral once served as a trade center for the region, which extends from the rainforests of the Amazon to the high forests of the Andes. Shady found evidence of a rich trading environment, including seeds of the cocoa bush and necklaces of shells, neither of which was native to the immediate Caral area. This environment gave rise to people who did not take part in the production of food, allowing them to become priests and planners, builders and designers. Thus occupational specialization elemental to an urban society, emerged.


F


But what sustained such a trading center and drew travelers to it? Was it food? Shady and her team found the bones of small edible fish, which must have come from the Pacific coast to the west, in the excavations. But they also found evidence of squash, sweet potatoes beans having been grown locally. Shady theorized that Caral's early farmers diverted the area's rivers into canals, which still cross the Supe Valley today, to irrigate their fields. But because she found no traces of maize, which can be traded or stored and used in times of crop failure, she concluded that Caral's trade leverage was not based on stockpiling food supplies.


G


It was evidence of another crop in the excavations that gave Shady the best clue to Caral's success. In nearly every excavated building, her team discovered evidence of cotton seeds, fibers and textiles. Her theory fell into place when a large fishing net made of those fibers, unearthed in an unrelated dig on Peru's coast turned out to be as old as Caral. 'The farmers of Caral grew the cotton that the fishermen needed to make their nets,' Shady speculates. 'And the fishermen gave them shellfish and dried fish in exchange for these nets.' In essence, the people of Caral enabled fishermen to work with larger and more effective nets, which made the resources of the sea more readily available, and the fishermen probably used dried squash grown by the Caral people as flotation devices for their nets.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1 - 6


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

1

Caral was built at the same time as the construction of the Egyptian pyramids.


正确答案： B. FALSE

定位词: Caral, built, Egyptian pyramids


对应原文 : B段, Research undertaken by Peruvian archaeologist Ruth Shady suggests that the 150-acre complex of pyramids, plazas and residential buildings was a thriving metropolis when Egypt's great pyramids were still being built.


详细解析 : B段指出，当埃及金字塔仍在建造时，Caral 已经是一个繁荣的都市了，这表明 Caral 和埃及金字塔的建设时间不是同时期。

2

The absence of pottery at the archaeological dig gave Shady a significant clue to the age of the site.


正确答案： A. TRUE

定位词 : absence of pottery, clue, age of the site


对应原文: C段, Shady and her crew searched for broken remains of the pots and containers that most such sites contain. Not finding any only made her more excited; it meant Caral could be what archaeologists term pre-ceramic, that is, existing before the advent in the area of pot-firing techniques.


详细解析 : C段指出，Shady 并没有发现任何陶器，反而让她更加兴奋，因为这表明 Caral 可能是“pre-ceramic（前陶器时代）”的。这一细节提示了 Caral 遗址的年代，


同义替换：absence= not finding any


3

The stones used to build Piramide Mayor came from a location far away.


正确答案： B. FALSE

定位词 : stones, Piramide Mayor, far away


对应原文 : C段, The original workers, she surmised, must have filled these bags with stones from a nearby quarry and laid them atop one another inside retaining walls, gradually giving rise to the pyramids immense structure. 


详细解析 : C段指出，建造 Piramide Mayor 的石头来自“附近的采石场（nearby quarry）”，而不是远处的地方。


4

The huge and complicated structures of Piramide Mayor suggest that its construction required an organised team of builders.


正确答案： A. TRUE

定位词 : huge and complicated structures, organised team of builders


对应原文 : D段, Thousands of manual laborers would have been needed to build such a project, not counting the many architects. craftsmen, and managers. 


详细解析 : D段指出，为了完成 Piramide Mayor 的建造需要成千上万的工人以及建筑师、工匠和管理者。这表明该工程需要一个有组织的团队。


同义替换：an organised team = thousands of manual laborers, architects, craftsmen, and managers

5

Archaeological evidence shows that the residents of Caral were highly skilled Musicians.


正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

定位词 : highly skilled musicians, archaeological evidence


对应原文 : D段, Shady's team found the remains of a large amphitheater, containing almost 70 musical instruments made of bird and deer bones.


详细解析 : D段指出发现了大量乐器，但并未说明 Caral 居民是否具备高超的音乐技艺。

6

The remains of housing areas at Caral suggest that there were no class distinctions in residential areas.


正确答案： B. FALSE

定位词: no class distinctions, residential areas


对应原文: D段, These indicate a hierarchy of living arrangements: large, well-kept rooms atop pyramids for the elite, ground-level quarters for craftsmen, and shabbier outlying dwellings for workers.


详细解析 :D段指出 Caral 的住宅区存在明显的阶级区分：精英住在金字塔顶层的优质房间，工匠住在地面房间，工人住在较差的外围房间。其中 "no class distinctions" 与 "a hierarchy of living arrangements" 形成对比。


Questions 7 - 13


Complete the notes. Write ONE WORD ONLY in each gap.

7

Caral as a trading centre


Items discovered at Caral but not naturally occurring in the area

• the  of a certain plant

•  used to make jewellery

• the remains of certain food such as .


Clues to farming around Caral

• still in existence today indicate water diverted from rivers

• no evidence that   was grown


Evidence of relationship with fishing communities

• the excavation findings and fishing nets found on the coast suggest Caral farmers traded  .

• dried squash may have been used to aid  of fishing nets


正确答案： 7 seeds  |  8 shells  |  9 fish  |  10 canals  |  11 maize  |  12 cotton  |  13 flotation
Question 7

定位词 : discovered at Caral, not naturally occurring, plant


对应原文 : E段, Shady found evidence of a rich trading environment, including seeds of the cocoa bush and necklaces of shells, neither of which was native to the immediate Caral area. 


详细解析: E段指出在 Caral 发现了可可树的“种子”（seeds），而该植物并非当地原生。这表明这些种子是从外地进口的贸易品。


同义替换：not naturally occurring in the area = evidence of a rich trading environment

Question 8

定位词 : discovered at Caral, not naturally occurring, used to make jewellery


对应原文 :E段, Shady found evidence of a rich trading environment, including seeds of the cocoa bush and necklaces of shells, neither of which was native to the immediate Caral area.


详细解析: E段指出在 Caral 发现了由“贝壳”（shells）制成的项链，而贝壳不是当地的原生材料。这表明贝壳是外来的交易物。


同义替换：used to make jewellery=necklaces of shells

Question 9

定位词 : remains of certain food, such as


对应原文 : F段, Shady and her team found the bones of small edible fish, which must have come from the Pacific coast to the west, in the excavations. 


详细解析 : F段指出，发现了小鱼的骨头，这些鱼类不属于当地。这表明鱼是 Caral 的一种重要食品来源。


同义替换：remains of certain food=bones of small edible fish

Question 10

定位词 : farming, still in existence, water diverted from rivers


对应原文 : F段, Shady theorized that Caral's early farmers diverted the area's rivers into canals, which still cross the Supe Valley today, to irrigate their fields. 


详细解析 : F段指出，Caral 早期的农民将河水引入“渠道”（canals），这些渠道今天仍然存在。


同义替换：still in existence =still cross the Supe Valley 

Question 11

定位词 : no evidence, grown


对应原文 : F段, But because she found no traces of maize, which can be traded or stored and used in times of crop failure, she concluded that Caral's trade leverage was not based on stockpiling food supplies.


详细解析 : F段指出，并未发现“玉米”（maize）的痕迹。


Question 12

定位词 : relationship with fishing communities, traded


对应原文:G段,  'The farmers of Caral grew the cotton that the fishermen needed to make their nets,' Shady speculates.


详细解析 : G段指出 Caral 的农民种植“棉花”（cotton），并将其与渔民交换。这表明棉花是重要的贸易品。


Question 13

定位词 : dried squash, aid, fishing nets


对应原文 : G段, In essence, the people of Caral enabled fishermen to work with larger and more effective nets, which made the resources of the sea more readily available, and the fishermen probably used dried squash grown by the Caral people as flotation devices for their nets.


详细解析 : G段指出渔民使用 Caral 人种植的干南瓜作为浮力装置，帮助网具浮起。
